problem_solved: Users lose context of their location in deep hierarchies
solution: Show clickable path trail from root to current page
when_to_use: Sites with 3+ hierarchy levels
when_not_to_use: Flat single-level sites or mobile-only apps
accessibility_impact: nav landmark with aria-label Breadcrumb and aria-current on last item
mobile_adaptation: Collapse to parent-only link or hide if space constrained
example_implementation: nav > ol > li with separator spans

problem_solved: Standard dropdowns cannot display large categorized navigation
solution: Full-width panel with grouped links columns and optional media
when_to_use: E-commerce and enterprise sites with 20+ nav items
when_not_to_use: Simple sites with fewer than 10 nav items
accessibility_impact: role=menu with keyboard arrow navigation and Escape to close
mobile_adaptation: Convert to full-screen category page or accordion
example_implementation: div with role=menu triggered by aria-expanded button

problem_solved: Multiple related content views compete for same space
solution: Horizontal tabs that switch visible content panel
when_to_use: 2-7 related views at same hierarchy level
when_not_to_use: More than 7 views or unrelated content
accessibility_impact: role=tablist with aria-selected and arrow key switching
mobile_adaptation: Scrollable tabs or convert to accordion on small screens
example_implementation: div role=tablist with button role=tab and div role=tabpanel

problem_solved: Primary navigation disappears on scroll losing quick access
solution: Header stays fixed at top of viewport during scroll
when_to_use: Long-scrolling pages where nav access is frequent
when_not_to_use: Short pages or pages with dedicated nav sidebar
accessibility_impact: Ensure sticky element does not cover focused elements
mobile_adaptation: Reduce header height on scroll or show mini bar
example_implementation: header with position sticky top 0 z-index

problem_solved: Users must scroll extensively to return to page top on long pages
solution: Floating button that scrolls to top on click
when_to_use: Pages longer than 4 viewport heights
when_not_to_use: Short pages or pages with sticky nav
accessibility_impact: aria-label Go to top and visible focus state
mobile_adaptation: Always show after 2 screens of scroll
example_implementation: Fixed button with smooth scroll to top behavior

problem_solved: Overwhelming users with too much information at once
solution: Show essential info first with option to reveal details on demand
when_to_use: Complex forms settings panels and information-dense pages
when_not_to_use: Simple content that fits comfortably without hiding
accessibility_impact: Disclosed content must be keyboard accessible and announced
mobile_adaptation: Accordions or expand links for secondary content
example_implementation: Details/summary elements or custom accordions

problem_solved: Pagination breaks content browsing flow in feed-style layouts
solution: Load more content automatically as user scrolls near bottom
when_to_use: Social feeds image galleries and discovery browsing
when_not_to_use: Content that needs direct page access or bookmarking
accessibility_impact: Announce new content loaded and manage focus for keyboard users
mobile_adaptation: Default pattern for mobile feeds
example_implementation: Intersection Observer triggers fetch and append

problem_solved: Blank page during load creates perception of slowness
solution: Show layout-matching placeholder shapes with pulse animation
when_to_use: Any async content load that takes 300ms+
when_not_to_use: Instant loads under 200ms or when progress is measurable
accessibility_impact: aria-busy=true on container and aria-label describing loading state
mobile_adaptation: Same skeleton layout adapted to mobile grid
example_implementation: Div with animated background-gradient matching content layout

problem_solved: Users see blank page with no context when data is absent
solution: Illustrative message explaining why empty and CTA to take action
when_to_use: Lists dashboards feeds and search results with no data
when_not_to_use: States that should never be empty in normal flow
accessibility_impact: Descriptive text not just illustration and actionable CTA with clear label
mobile_adaptation: Simplified illustration or icon with concise message
example_implementation: Container with illustration heading description and CTA button

problem_solved: User needs non-blocking confirmation of completed actions
solution: Brief auto-dismissing message at screen edge
when_to_use: Success confirmations non-critical warnings and info updates
when_not_to_use: Critical errors requiring user acknowledgment
accessibility_impact: role=status with aria-live=polite and optional action button
mobile_adaptation: Bottom of screen full width with swipe to dismiss
example_implementation: Fixed position container with role=status and auto-dismiss timer

problem_solved: Users encounter errors and do not know how to proceed
solution: Clear error message with specific guidance and recovery action
when_to_use: Form validation API errors and system failures
when_not_to_use: Scenarios where errors are impossible or pre-validated
accessibility_impact: Error messages associated via aria-describedby and not color-only
mobile_adaptation: Inline errors below field level with scroll-to-first-error
example_implementation: Alert with role=alert and actionable retry or fix guidance

problem_solved: Users type long queries or misspell common searches
solution: Show matching suggestions as user types with keyboard selection
when_to_use: Search fields with predictable or bounded result sets
when_not_to_use: Free-text areas or when suggestions would be misleading
accessibility_impact: role=combobox with aria-expanded aria-activedescendant and result count
mobile_adaptation: Full-screen search overlay on mobile with recent and trending
example_implementation: Combobox input with listbox results and debounced API calls

problem_solved: Users submit forms only to discover multiple errors at once
solution: Validate and show feedback as user completes each field
when_to_use: Forms with specific format requirements or common mistakes
when_not_to_use: Simple forms with 1-2 fields or when validation is slow
accessibility_impact: aria-invalid=true with aria-describedby linking to error message
mobile_adaptation: Same behavior with errors below fields
example_implementation: onBlur validation with adjacent error span

problem_solved: Long forms overwhelm users with too many fields at once
solution: Break form into logical steps with progress indicator
when_to_use: Forms with 8+ fields that group into logical sections
when_not_to_use: Short forms under 6 fields or forms needing cross-field comparison
accessibility_impact: Announce current step and total and preserve completed data on back
mobile_adaptation: Full-width steps with minimal progress indicator
example_implementation: Stepper component with fieldset per step and session storage

problem_solved: Labels take vertical space above inputs reducing form density
solution: Label starts as placeholder then animates above on focus
when_to_use: Dense forms where vertical space is premium
when_not_to_use: Forms requiring persistent visible labels for complex fields
accessibility_impact: Ensure label remains visible and associated via htmlFor at all states
mobile_adaptation: Same behavior works well on mobile touch
example_implementation: Input with label that transitions from inside to above via CSS

problem_solved: Users create weak passwords without feedback on quality
solution: Real-time visual indicator of password strength as user types
when_to_use: Registration and password change forms
when_not_to_use: Login forms or non-security-critical fields
accessibility_impact: aria-live region announcing strength level changes
mobile_adaptation: Same component works on mobile
example_implementation: Progress bar with aria-valuenow and descriptive level text

problem_solved: Manual reordering through form controls is tedious
solution: Allow direct manipulation reordering via mouse or touch drag
when_to_use: Lists kanban boards file uploads and layout builders
when_not_to_use: When order does not matter or when items exceed 20
accessibility_impact: Keyboard alternative required via arrow keys with grab/drop announcements
mobile_adaptation: Long-press to initiate drag with haptic feedback
example_implementation: Draggable items with keyboard listeners and aria-grabbed

problem_solved: Action buttons for each list item clutter the interface
solution: Show actions on hover or via three-dot menu per item
when_to_use: Lists and tables where items have 2-5 possible actions
when_not_to_use: Single-action items or when all actions must be visible
accessibility_impact: Menu button with aria-haspopup and keyboard-accessible menu items
mobile_adaptation: Three-dot menu always visible on mobile
example_implementation: Button with aria-haspopup=menu triggering dropdown menu

problem_solved: Users want control over light and dark color schemes
solution: Toggle switch that swaps CSS custom properties for theme
when_to_use: Any app or site used for extended periods especially dev tools
when_not_to_use: Print-focused sites or sites with brand-critical color schemes
accessibility_impact: Ensure all contrast ratios maintained in both themes
mobile_adaptation: Same toggle often in settings or header
example_implementation: Toggle button setting class on html and persisting to localStorage

problem_solved: Power users need fast access to actions without menu navigation
solution: Keyboard-triggered searchable action list
when_to_use: Developer tools productivity apps and complex SaaS dashboards
when_not_to_use: Simple sites with few actions or non-technical audiences
accessibility_impact: role=combobox with results as role=option and keyboard navigation
mobile_adaptation: Usually omitted on mobile or triggered via dedicated button
example_implementation: Cmd+K triggered modal with search input and action list

problem_solved: Waiting for server response makes interface feel slow
solution: Update UI immediately assuming success then reconcile on response
when_to_use: Common fast operations like likes toggles and simple edits
when_not_to_use: Critical operations like payments or destructive deletes
accessibility_impact: Announce state change immediately then announce error if rollback
mobile_adaptation: Same approach works identically on mobile
example_implementation: Update local state immediately with rollback handler on API error

problem_solved: Displaying multiple similar items in scannable format
solution: Uniform cards in responsive grid with consistent information hierarchy
when_to_use: Product catalogs team directories feature overviews
when_not_to_use: Content requiring sequential reading or deep comparison
accessibility_impact: Cards as article or li with proper heading and image alt
mobile_adaptation: Reduce columns 4 to 2 to 1 as viewport shrinks
example_implementation: CSS Grid with auto-fill minmax and card component

problem_solved: Primary action needs to be prominent but alternatives exist
solution: Button with default action and dropdown for alternatives
when_to_use: When one action is used 80%+ of the time with occasional alternatives
when_not_to_use: When all actions are equally likely
accessibility_impact: Primary button and dropdown trigger have separate aria-labels
mobile_adaptation: Full-width button with secondary action row below
example_implementation: Button group with primary and dropdown-trigger sharing visual container

problem_solved: Destructive or irreversible actions need explicit user consent
solution: Modal dialog requiring explicit confirm or cancel
when_to_use: Delete operations payments and irreversible state changes
when_not_to_use: Routine non-destructive operations
accessibility_impact: role=alertdialog with aria-describedby explaining consequences
mobile_adaptation: Same dialog adapted to bottom sheet on mobile
example_implementation: Dialog with clear destructive action label and escape to cancel

problem_solved: Table rows need to show additional details without navigation
solution: Clicking row expands inline detail panel below the row
when_to_use: Master-detail data where details are secondary to list scanning
when_not_to_use: When all detail fields are equally important and should show in columns
accessibility_impact: aria-expanded on trigger row and aria-controls linking to detail panel
mobile_adaptation: Detail panel becomes full card view on mobile
example_implementation: tr with aria-expanded toggling adjacent tr containing detail content
